title: Anacamptis × caccabaria
taxonomy: Anacamptis × caccabaria is a hybrid of the orchid species Anacamptis pyramidalis and Anacamptis palustris. This hybrid is part of the Orchidaceae family and is commonly found in the Mediterranean region. It is often classified as a subspecies of Anacamptis pyramidalis, with the scientific name Anacamptis × caccabaria being used to distinguish it from its parent species. The classification of Anacamptis × caccabaria is still a subject of debate among botanists, with some considering it a distinct species. Further research is needed to fully understand the taxonomic classification of this unique orchid hybrid.
uses: Anacamptis × caccabaria is known to be a hybrid orchid species and, as such, its medicinal uses are not well-documented. However, its parent species, Anacamptis papillata and Anacamptis coriophora, have been used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits.
